evals.prompt: Verify the sustainability report I just generated with /gc-setup
evals.expected_output: Runs full 8-check adversarial verification on the most recent gc-* report in conversation.
assertions: Checks all data points for cited sources
Challenges default assumptions (device power, carbon intensity, etc.)
Flags any false precision in reported numbers
Identifies what was NOT measured (coverage gaps)

evals.prompt: Our website emits 0.5g CO2 per page view. We claim to be a sustainable website. Is this accurate?
evals.expected_output: Verifies the claim: checks source for 0.5g figure, challenges scope and methodology, flags 'sustainable' as unqualified claim.
assertions: Asks for the source and methodology behind 0.5g
Flags 'sustainable website' as potential greenwashing without qualification
Checks if scope includes all system components (CDN, third-party, backend)

evals.prompt: We reduced our SCI from 25 mgCO2eq to 12 mgCO2eq per API call after optimization. Can you verify this improvement?
evals.expected_output: Verifies both measurements: same methodology, same conditions, appropriate precision, and whether the improvement exceeds measurement noise.
assertions: Checks if both measurements used the same methodology and conditions
Verifies the improvement exceeds measurement uncertainty
Checks if functional unit definition is consistent
Asks about what changed between measurements
